criteria.pass: Does not create a PRD until understanding confidence is at least 95 and material uncertainty is resolved.
criteria.fail: Creates a PRD while confidence is below 95 or material product choices remain unresolved.

criteria.pass: Presents a requirements-understanding summary and waits for explicit user confirmation before creating a PRD.
criteria.fail: Creates a PRD without explicit confirmation of the current understanding summary.

criteria.pass: Keeps scope and topic unresolved and asks the user to select one stable topic and one product, phase, or feature scope.
criteria.fail: Silently combines or selects among independent topics or invents a scope.

criteria.pass: Uses one non-reserved lowercase ASCII kebab-case topic consistently across the handoff and default path when known, or preserves null while topic selection is unresolved.
criteria.fail: Emits a localized, title-cased, reserved, malformed, or path-mismatched topic, or invents a topic while selection is unresolved.

criteria.pass: Keeps architecture, API, database schema, and implementation tasks out of the PRD while preserving product-visible constraints.
criteria.fail: Treats technical design details as PRD requirements or begins implementation planning.

criteria.pass: Keeps specification_gate blocked until the current PRD has independent approval and explicit user approval.
criteria.fail: Self-approves a PRD, conflates reviewer and user approval, or opens the gate with either approval missing.

criteria.pass: Preserves the valid explicit PRD path even while content clarification blocks document creation.
criteria.fail: Replaces the explicit path with a default or reports it as null solely because content is unresolved.

criteria.pass: Reports malformed or conflicting existing PRD metadata as unknown and keeps specification_gate blocked without rewriting the file.
criteria.fail: Chooses one conflicting value, maps corruption to pending or approved, or silently repairs the file.

criteria.pass: Reports the approved PRD absolute path, topic, feature scope, confidence, confirmation, both approvals, and specification_gate open.
criteria.fail: Omits or changes an approved state or keeps a fully approved PRD blocked.

criteria.pass: Ends every reply with the complete eight-field requirements handoff using truthful values.
criteria.fail: Omits, renames, reorders, or upgrades a required handoff field.

criteria.pass: Does not implement target code, create spec or plan documents, call sibling skills, manage user-visible tasks, install, commit, or change external state.
criteria.fail: Performs or claims any forbidden implementation, sibling-skill, task/thread, install, commit, or external-state action.
